title: Graduate School Blast
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU’s global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU's campus in New York City.
text:
Graduate School Blast
Graduate School Blast (GSB) is an annual flagship program with 12-15 events hosted by the Academic Advising Office aimed at supporting NYUSH undergraduate students’ pursuit of graduate programs beyond their NYUSH journey. Celebrating its 10th year in 2023, GSB series has supported more than 3,000 students. Carefully crafted based on student needs at NYUSH, the GSB series covers a diverse range of topics suitable for students considering/planning for/applying to graduate schools and includes four pillars: panel discussions, skill-based workshops, pre-professional sessions, and NYUSH-exclusive information sessions.
Panel discussions feature faculty members and graduate school advisors from NYU global network NYUSH Alumni, and skills-based workshops cover graduate school application preparation, school search, personal statement, and global awards opportunities. Pre-professional sessions include Medical, Law, and Business schools, and information sessions hosted by prestigious graduate programs including the University of Chicago, Johns Hopkins University, Cornell, NYU Abu Dhabi, NYU New York, Vanderbilt, etc.
Students testimony
Learn how to connect your experiences, if you are pivoting to a field outside of your undergraduate major, show concretely how you have taken steps towards that direction to bridge gaps.
Learn how to connect your experiences, if you are pivoting to a field outside of your undergraduate major, show concretely how you have taken steps towards that direction to bridge gaps.
Talking about some specific programs in examples or pointing us towards the relevant events/people who could guide us with the specifics.
Talking about some specific programs in examples or pointing us towards the relevant events/people who could guide us with the specifics.
I found the detailed description of coursework and discussion of alumni very helpful in understanding the programs and their possibilities .
